title: Will June CPI inflation be above 3.0%?
description: Binary market tracking whether the June 2026 all-items Consumer Price Index year-over-year reading is strictly greater than 3.0%.
rules: Resolves Yes if the first scheduled Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI release for June 2026 reports an all-items year-over-year value greater than 3.0%. Revisions after the first release are ignored.
resolutionSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI release
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not live Kalshi data and not trading advice.

title: Will the Fed cut rates at the September meeting?
description: Binary market tracking whether the Federal Open Market Committee lowers the target range for the federal funds rate at its September 2026 meeting.
rules: Resolves Yes if the FOMC statement released at the scheduled September 2026 meeting announces a lower target range than the range in effect immediately before the meeting.
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; no interest-rate forecast or trading recommendation is implied.

title: Will July unemployment be below 4.0%?
description: Binary market tracking whether the July 2026 U-3 unemployment rate is reported below 4.0%.
rules: Resolves Yes if the first scheduled Bureau of Labor Statistics Employment Situation report for July 2026 shows U-3 unemployment below 4.0%. Revisions are ignored.
resolutionSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics Employment Situation release
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not live Kalshi data.

title: Will July nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000?
description: Binary market tracking whether total nonfarm payroll employment increases by more than 200,000 jobs in the July 2026 jobs report.
rules: Resolves Yes if the first scheduled Employment Situation report for July 2026 reports a total nonfarm payroll change greater than 200,000. Revisions are ignored.
resolutionSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics Employment Situation release
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not a payroll forecast.

title: Will Q2 US GDP growth be above 2.5%?
description: Binary market tracking whether the advance estimate of Q2 2026 real GDP annualized growth is above 2.5%.
rules: Resolves Yes if the Bureau of Economic Analysis advance estimate for Q2 2026 real GDP annualized growth is greater than 2.5%. Later revisions are ignored.
resolutionSource: Bureau of Economic Analysis GDP advance estimate
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not an economic forecast.

title: Will weekly jobless claims exceed 250,000?
description: Binary market tracking whether seasonally adjusted initial unemployment insurance claims exceed 250,000 in a specified weekly release.
rules: Resolves Yes if the Department of Labor weekly release for the target week reports seasonally adjusted initial claims greater than 250,000.
resolutionSource: Department of Labor weekly unemployment insurance claims release
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not live Kalshi data.

title: Will WTI oil close above 90 dollars on July 31?
description: Binary market tracking whether a referenced WTI crude oil benchmark closing price is above 90 dollars on July 31, 2026.
rules: Resolves Yes if the selected WTI crude oil settlement or closing benchmark for July 31, 2026 is strictly greater than 90.00 dollars.
resolutionSource: Referenced WTI crude oil settlement publisher
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; no commodity trading advice is implied.

title: Will Henry Hub natural gas close above 4 dollars?
description: Binary market tracking whether a referenced Henry Hub natural gas futures settlement is above 4.00 dollars on a specified date.
rules: Resolves Yes if the referenced Henry Hub natural gas settlement price for the target contract date is strictly greater than 4.00 dollars.
resolutionSource: Referenced Henry Hub natural gas settlement publisher
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not live commodity data.

title: Will Bitcoin be above 100,000 dollars on December 31?
description: Binary market tracking whether the reference Bitcoin spot price is above 100,000 dollars at a specified year-end observation time.
rules: Resolves Yes if the selected Bitcoin reference price is strictly greater than 100,000 dollars at the stated December 31, 2026 observation time.
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; no crypto trading advice is implied.

title: Will Ethereum be above 6,000 dollars on December 31?
description: Binary market tracking whether the reference Ethereum spot price is above 6,000 dollars at a specified year-end observation time.
rules: Resolves Yes if the selected Ethereum reference price is strictly greater than 6,000 dollars at the stated December 31, 2026 observation time.
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not live market data.

title: Will presidential approval be above 50% on August 1?
description: Binary market tracking whether a named national polling average reports presidential approval above 50% on August 1, 2026.
rules: Resolves Yes if the selected polling-average snapshot published for August 1, 2026 reports approval strictly greater than 50.0%.
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not a political forecast.

title: Will statewide election turnout exceed 60%?
description: Binary market tracking whether certified turnout in a fictional statewide election exceeds 60% of registered voters.
rules: Resolves Yes if certified election results report turnout strictly greater than 60.0% of registered voters for the specified statewide contest.
notes: Fictional civic-event market for RAG demonstration only.

title: Will New York City rainfall exceed two inches over Memorial Day weekend?
description: Binary market tracking whether measured rainfall at a referenced New York City station exceeds two inches during a defined weekend window.
rules: Resolves Yes if the official measurement station records cumulative rainfall greater than 2.00 inches during the stated observation window.
resolutionSource: Official New York City weather station rainfall report
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; weather outcomes are sample data.

title: Will a named hurricane make Florida landfall before October?
description: Binary market tracking whether a named Atlantic hurricane makes landfall in Florida before October 1, 2026.
rules: Resolves Yes if an official tropical cyclone report identifies a named Atlantic hurricane landfall in Florida before October 1, 2026.
resolutionSource: National Hurricane Center tropical cyclone reports
notes: Fictional quote snapshot for RAG demonstration only; not weather or insurance advice.

title: Will Metro City win the soccer championship?
description: Binary market tracking whether a fictional Metro City soccer club wins a fictional championship final.
rules: Resolves Yes if Metro City is the official winner after regulation, extra time, or penalties in the specified championship final.
notes: Fictional sports market for retrieval variety; teams and event are not real.

title: Will Coastal City win the pro basketball title?
description: Binary market tracking whether a fictional Coastal City basketball team wins a fictional pro basketball title.
rules: Resolves Yes if Coastal City is listed as the official champion after the final game of the specified fictional basketball season.
notes: Fictional sports market for RAG demonstration only.

title: Will an animated movie open above 100 million dollars?
description: Binary market tracking whether a fictional animated movie exceeds 100 million dollars in domestic opening weekend box office.
rules: Resolves Yes if the selected box office reporting estimate for opening weekend domestic gross is strictly greater than 100 million dollars.
resolutionSource: Selected domestic box office reporting estimate
notes: Fictional entertainment market for RAG demonstration only.

title: Will Harbor Lights win best picture?
description: Binary market tracking whether the fictional film Harbor Lights wins a fictional best picture award.
rules: Resolves Yes if Harbor Lights is announced as the winner of the specified best picture award at the fictional ceremony.
notes: Fictional entertainment market for RAG demonstration only; no real awards data is represented.
